chunks.text: If I were a bird, I would fly freely wherever I wished.
In the morning I would pass through the forest
at noon I would cross the valley
and at night I would sleep beside the stars.
If I had wings, I would soar above the clouds.
I would not feel the distance of far cities.
If I were not bound to the ground
I would surely travel wherever the wind blew.
Yet, now I stand on the earth.
Still, to imagine is a small form of freedom.
Looking up at the evening sky, I think: if I were a bird
how deeply would I admire this sky?
Standing on the ground, I still look up at the same sky.
